title: Idempotency, Inbox Transactions, and Effects · Execution Trace: Transactional Inbox
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must validate stable identity and fingerprint, compose inbox and domain mutation, and preserve external-effect ambiguity; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transactional inbox increment in a duplicate-safe Go effect state machine. Learners progress through…
objectives: Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
